egg.notes: Flax egg: 1 tbsp ground flaxseed + 3 tbsp water. Tofu scramble works for savoury dishes. Banana/applesauce for baking.

onion.notes: Shallots are milder. Leeks work well in soups. Celery adds crunch without strong flavour.

tomato.notes: Passata gives smooth consistency. Sun-dried tomatoes are more concentrated in flavour.

garlic.notes: Use 1/4 tsp garlic powder per clove. Asafoetida is common in Indian cooking as a garlic substitute.

rice.notes: Cauliflower rice is low-carb. Quinoa adds more protein. Couscous cooks faster.

pasta.notes: Zucchini noodles (courgetti) are low-carb. Rice noodles are gluten-free.

bread.notes: Sourdough is easier to digest. Naan and pita work well for dipping.

milk.notes: Oat milk is best for cooking (neutral flavour). Coconut milk adds richness to curries.

cheese.notes: Nutritional yeast gives a cheesy flavour. Cashew cream works for sauces.

pepper.notes: Courgette adds similar bulk. Mushrooms add umami depth.

potato.notes: Sweet potato adds natural sweetness. Cauliflower is low-carb alternative for mash.

butter.notes: Olive oil works for most savoury cooking. Coconut oil works for baking.

cream.notes: Coconut cream is thick and rich. Cashew cream is neutral-tasting.

soy_sauce.notes: Tamari is gluten-free. Coconut aminos is lower sodium.

sugar.notes: Honey and maple syrup add distinct flavours. Reduce liquid slightly when substituting.

flour.notes: Almond flour is high-protein. Coconut flour is very absorbent — use less.

stock.notes: Miso paste adds umami. A bouillon cube dissolved in water works in a pinch.

olive_oil.notes: Avocado oil has a high smoke point for frying. Coconut oil adds slight sweetness.
